message:
fix(net): enforce overall timeout on single-address happy eyeballs path

The single-address fast path in connect_with_time_getter previously
called connect_one directly, bypassing the overall_timeout that the
multi-address racing path enforces. If the per-connection timeout was
longer than the overall timeout, a single-address connection could
exceed the expected maximum wait time.

New connect_single and connect_single_with_connector functions wrap
the connection attempt in future_with_timeout using the overall_timeout
deadline. Zero overall_timeout returns TimedOut immediately. The
timeout message is extracted to a constant (OVERALL_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MSG)
shared with the RaceConnections path.

connect_single_with_connector is generic over the connector function,
enabling testing without real network I/O. Test added: verifies that a
single-address connection attempt against a permanently-pending connector
times out when the injected clock advances past the overall deadline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
